ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger class (FOODON_00002383). No specific triple cheeseburger with mushroom sauce class exists; closest_parent match used. The set meal component (fries and drink) is not captured in this single ontology node.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FAN covers hamburgers and similar products. No specific triple cheeseburger with creamy mushroom sauce and set meal code exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for a composite fast food burger set meal. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising a triple cheeseburger with creamy mushroom sauce, a portion of Star Potatoes (fries), and a soft drink, estimated at approximately 550 g total weight.

nutrient_source.title: Creamy Mushroom Triple Cheeseburger Special — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for the Creamy Mushroom Triple Cheeseburger Special (cr_id G00010101), confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB record and are set to -1 where unavailable.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on white bread bun (GI ~71–75) and French fries (GI ~63–75) composite
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded set meal. GI estimated at 72 based on the dominant carbohydrate-contributing components: refined white burger bun (GI ~71–75, University of Sydney GI Database) and deep-fried potato fries (GI ~63–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The soft drink component (sugar-sweetened beverage) further elevates the effective glycemic response. A composite GI of 72 (high category) is defensible for this meal. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 162.6 g carbohydrate in 550 g serving at 29.57 g/100g) / 100 ≈ 116.9. This is a very high GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100g) with low potassium offset (197 mg/100g); triple processed cheese and creamy mushroom sauce add further sodium; sugar-sweetened beverage component
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal due to its high sodium density (487 mg/100g), unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio, and the additional sodium burden from triple processed cheese and creamy sauce; if consumed, request no soft drink, sauce on the side, and limit portion to a half serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~72) with 29.57 g carbohydrate/100g and only 1.8 g dietary fibre/100g; sugar-sweetened beverage causes rapid postprandial glucose spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: This meal is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycaemic index (~72), low fibre content (1.8 g/100g), and the inclusion of a sugar-sweetened soft drink; substituting the soft drink with water, replacing fries with a side salad, and reducing portion size are the minimum modifications required.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Triple processed cheese and creamy sauce contribute significant saturated fat; high sodium (487 mg/100g); cholesterol 29 mg/100g; deep-fried fries add trans fat and oxidised lipids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal poses a high cardiovascular risk due to its saturated fat load from triple cheese and creamy mushroom sauce, elevated sodium content, and deep-fried potato component generating oxidised lipids and potential trans fats; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal or limit consumption to rare occasions with significant modifications.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef patties provide arachidonic acid (omega-6) amplifying inflammatory pathways; deep-fried fries generate advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); triple cheese adds saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal, particularly during flares, as the triple beef patties supply pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid, the deep-fried fries generate AGEs that worsen joint inflammation, and the saturated fat from triple cheese further promotes systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Triple Cheeseburger Special is a Western-style fast food set meal sold in Singapore, comprising a triple beef patty cheeseburger topped with a creamy mushroom sauce and three slices of processed cheese, served with a portion of deep-fried potato fries (Star Potatoes) and a sugar-sweetened soft drink. The burger bun is a refined white bread roll, contributing the majority of the meal's carbohydrate load. The triple beef patties are grilled and provide substantial protein (13.3 g/100g) and fat (10.18 g/100g). The creamy mushroom sauce adds richness, saturated fat, and additional sodium beyond the stated per-100g values. Per 100 g, the meal delivers 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, 29.57 g carbohydrate, 5.95 g sugar, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 487 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high category), driven by the refined bun and deep-fried fries. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, high GI, low fibre, and significant saturated fat content. It is a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-GI meal appropriate only for occasional consumption by healthy adults. The dish is culturally positioned as a Western fast food indulgence within the Singapore food landscape.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010101) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on the dominant carbohydrate components (refined white bun and deep-fried fries), which is the standard defensible approach when no direct GI measurement exists for a branded composite meal.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this item (G00010101) as of 2026-04-16. The input record notes an estimated trans fat of ~0.2 g/100g from the deep-fried fries component, but this is an estimate and not a confirmed HPB-reported value; it is therefore not entered as a numeric value and is documented here only. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Mixed' to 'Mixed / Fast food meal / burger set' as this is a composite set meal dish, not a baking ingredient. The halal status of this specific product is not confirmed in the HPB record and is set to false pending verification. Glycaemic load per serving calculated using estimated serving size of 550 g and carbohydrate content of 29.57 g/100g: GL = (72 × 162.6) / 100 ≈ 116.9.
